findings.message: get_stock now reads data["reserved_count"] from the inventory response, but the consumer Pact interaction in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py (will_respond_with body {"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5)}) does not declare reserved_count. The consumer contract therefore does not pin a field the consumer now hard-depends on: the published pact tells the provider only in_stock/item_id are needed, so provider verification stays green even if the provider never sends (or later drops) reserved_count, while the real client raises KeyError. This is an uncovered consumer dependency / weakened contract for reserved_count.
findings.suggestion: Add reserved_count to the consumer pact interaction so the contract covers the field the client reads, e.g. .with_body({"item_id": match.int(1), "in_stock": match.int(5), "reserved_count": match.int(0)}), and update the test assertion accordingly; then regenerate and publish the pact.
